Activity: Jenna Williams shared this
...
🚀 Exciting News from Our Analytics & AI Team at Brown-Forman! 🚀
...
Check out a recent feature in Fortune where our director, Amit Parulekar , discusses the innovative steps we're taking in AI at Brown-Forman . As part of the Global Advanced Analytics & AI team, it's exciting to see our work spotlighted in such a prestigious outlet.
...
He mentions our cautious yet ambitious approach to AI, emphasizing that "
“What we are doing is carefully curating the use cases, accelerating and investing where necessary, but also piloting new technologies, such as generative AI.” 

This philosophy guides our dual strategy for integrating AI across Brown-Forman, focusing on both quick wins to showcase AI's immediate benefits and committing to long-term initiatives that promise to transform how we operate.
...
Our journey includes developing 200,000 elasticity models to optimize pricing and promotions globally, enhancing whiskey production through color accuracy AI models, and leveraging machine learning to forecast demand. These efforts are part of our broader mission to responsibly implement AI, ensuring each use case aligns with our company's values and vision.
...
Piloting technologies, like generative AI, highlights our commitment to innovation and the exciting future of AI in our industry.
...
I invite you to read the full article to learn more about how we're paving the way for AI in the spirits industry and beyond. Let's toast to the future! 🥃 #AI #Innovation #BrownForman #FortuneMagazine #DataScience #MachineLearning #GenerativeAI #Leadership

Seasoned supply chain, master data, and enterprise systems leader with a long‑standing record of shaping global operational standards at Brown‑Forman. I specialize in building the processes, data foundations, and cross‑functional alignment that enable a complex global business to operate with clarity, consistency, and strategic intent.My career spans more than three decades of progressive leadership across supply chain, finance, logistics, manufacturing systems, and SAP architecture. This breadth gives me a uniquely holistic view of how data, process, and technology intersect — and how to design enterprise solutions that scale.Today I lead Master Data Excellence and Operational Intelligence, driving the governance, quality, and strategic use of master data across Brown‑Forman’s global footprint. I focus on elevating data as a business asset, strengthening operational insights, and ensuring that our systems and processes support growth, compliance, and long‑term resilience.Known for collaborative leadership, strategic thinking, and the ability to translate complex business needs into sustainable, technology‑enabled solutions. Activity: Patrick George liked this
...
This might sound like a hot take, but it’s not for anyone deep into the weeds of AI right now... Most people don’t actually want "AI Agents." They want AI workflows.
...
Most business use cases of AI are not going to be solved by an AI agent. They will be solved by AI workflows that mimic and automate existing processes that humans do manually today. There is no need to recreate the wheel or leave planning up to an AI agent when a business process is well defined.
...
There is also a major concern and challenge with accuracy and consistency of outputs with agents. At the enterprise level, organizations will almost always want workflows over agents when the stakes are high and there is compliance involved.
...
Today, many people are calling AI workflows “AI Agents” but under the hood there is pre-defined planning, prompts, tools, and data retrieval pipelines. This is an important distinction that matters. 

At Valent, we have built a platform and framework for building and deploying custom AI workflows that are purpose built to automate a significant amount of work for RFP responses teams. This framework leverages the outputs of AI workflows to build user interfaces for teams to collaborate with. It also leverages the outputs from workflows to help manage the context of our autonomous chat agent our users can utilize to cover the long tail of use cases that are not already covered by workflows or our user interface. 

The future will be a mixture/balance of approaches but the foreseeable future will mostly be AI workflows. 

The progression will be:

Workflows ——> Vertical Agents ——> Agents

The Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI will apply knowledge and experience to real world problems and seek to utilize their skills reduce the cost of healthcare and improve health quality and outcomes. With access to dedicated on premise and cloud based big data solutions, the team can work with a vast amount of structured and unstructured data including claims, membership, physician demographics, medical records and others to begin to solve some of the most pressing healthcare issues of our time. A Data Scientist at Cotiviti will be given the opportunity to work directly with a team of healthcare professionals including analysts, clinicians, coding specialists, auditors and innovators to set aggressive goals and execute on them with the team. This is for an ambitious technologist, with the flexibility and personal drive to succeed in a dynamic environment where they are judged based on their direct impact to business outcomes.

- As a Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI within Cotiviti you will be responsible for delivering solutions that help our clients identify payment integrity issues, reduce the cost of healthcare processes, or improve the quality of healthcare outcomes. You will work as part of a team and will be individually responsible for the delivery of value associated with your projects. You will be expected to follow processes and practices that allow your models to be incorporated into our machine learning platform for production execution and monitoring, however, initial exploratory data analysis allows for more flexible experimentation to discover solutions to the business problems presented.

...
Distinguished individual contributor role leading next-generation agentic AI systems and autonomous agents at enterprise scale across Walmart's global operations, combining deep ML/AI expertise with cross-functional mentorship and high-impact productionized solutions.

We are seeking a Distinguished Data Scientist to provide technical leadership and long‑term vision for next‑generation AI systems and platforms. This senior individual contributor will drive breakthrough system design and advanced solution development that transforms Walmart at scale. The role combines deep technical expertise (ML, simulation, optimization, agentic AI) with cross‑functional influence, mentoring of senior talent, and delivery of high‑impact, productionized solutions. You will be a great fit if: - You architect end-to-end ML/AI systems — from feature engineering through production deployment and monitoring - You’ve built and shipped multiple autonomous AI agents (not just experimented with LLM APIs) - You’re Level 5+ on Steve Yegge’s Vibe Coding scale and can ship production systems through AI-assisted development - You combine deep statistical rigor with practical business impact

You’ll build production Agentic systems, deploy autonomous AI agents, and translate complex data into actionable insights that drive measurable business value at massive scale. Our team builds intelligent platforms, agents and ML-powered solutions that make associates’ jobs easier, faster, and more fulfilling across stores, fulfillment centers, distribution centers, and home office. We leverage the Element LLM Gateway to safely access state-of-the-art language models while keeping all data within Walmart’s network. Our work spans NLP, computer vision, time series forecasting, and multi-agent AI systems at enterprise scale.
